NCT00305162.name: CHAMPION-PCI
NCT00305162.group: Elective/ACS PCI, cangrelor vs clopidogrel 600 mg
NCT00305162.snippet: Source: Harrington RA et al. NEJM 2009;361:2318-2329 (CHAMPION-PCI).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Harrington RA et al. NEJM 2009;361:2318-2329, CONSORT
evidence.text: 8,877 patients undergoing PCI (elective or ACS) were randomized to IV cangrelor periprocedurally plus clopidogrel 600 mg at end of procedure (n=4,459) vs clopidogrel 600 mg at start of procedure (n=4,466).

evidence.label: Primary — 48h Composite
evidence.source: Harrington RA et al. NEJM 2009, Table 2
evidence.text: Primary endpoint (death, MI, ischaemia-driven revasc at 48h) occurred in 7.5% (335/4459) cangrelor vs 7.1% (317/4466) clopidogrel. OR 1.05 (95% CI 0.88-1.24; P=0.59). Non-inferior but not superior. Trial stopped early for futility.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "INCLUSION CRITERIA~To be included in this study, subjects must meet the following criteria:~* Angiography demonstrating atherosclerosis amenable to treatment by percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) with or without stent implantation and diagnosis of Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) by elevated cardiac markers or ischemic chest discomfort w/electrocardiogram changes + age > 65 or diabetes or ST-elevation MI.~EXCLUSION CRITERIA~Subjects will be excluded from the study if they present with any o...

NCT00385138.name: CHAMPION-PLATFORM
NCT00385138.group: Elective/ACS PCI, cangrelor vs placebo then clopidogrel 600
NCT00385138.snippet: Source: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2009;361:2330-2341 (CHAMPION-PLATFORM).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2009;361:2330-2341, CONSORT
evidence.text: 5,362 patients undergoing PCI not on prior thienopyridine were randomized to IV cangrelor (n=2,654) vs placebo (n=2,641), all receiving clopidogrel 600 mg at end of PCI.

evidence.label: Primary — 48h Composite
evidence.text: Primary endpoint at 48h: 7.0% cangrelor vs 8.0% placebo. OR 0.87 (95% CI 0.71-1.07; P=0.17). Non-significant; stopped early for futility. Secondary stent thrombosis reduced (0.2% vs 0.6%; OR 0.31, P=0.02).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~Angiography demonstrating atherosclerosis amenable to treatment by percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) with or without stent implantation and diagnosis of Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) by elevated cardiac markers or ischemic chest discomfort w/electrocardiogram changes + age > 65 or diabetes.~Exclusion Criteria:~1. Not a candidate for PCI~2. ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) within 48 hours of randomization~3. Increased bleeding risk: ischemic stroke w...

NCT01156571.name: CHAMPION-PHOENIX
NCT01156571.group: Elective/ACS PCI, cangrelor vs clopidogrel 300/600 mg

allOutcomes.title: 48-hour composite (death, MI, ischaemia-driven revasc, stent thrombosis)

NCT01156571.snippet: Source: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2013;368:1303-1313 (CHAMPION-PHOENIX).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2013;368:1303-1313, CONSORT
evidence.text: 11,145 patients undergoing elective or urgent PCI were randomized to IV cangrelor (n=5,472) vs clopidogrel 300 or 600 mg (n=5,470). Patients were thienopyridine-naïve pre-PCI.

evidence.label: Primary — 48h Composite
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. NEJM 2013, Table 2
evidence.text: Primary 48h composite (death, MI, ischaemia-driven revascularization, stent thrombosis): 4.7% (257/5,472) cangrelor vs 5.9% (322/5,470) clopidogrel. OR 0.78 (95% CI 0.66-0.93; P=0.005). Stent thrombosis reduced (0.8% vs 1.4%; OR 0.62, P=0.01).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Cardiology | Cangrelor vs Clopidogrel in PCI v1.0
